content: The design shear strength of a reinforced concrete rectangular beam with a width of 250 mm and an effective depth of 500 mm , is 0.3 MPa . The torsional moment capacity of the section (in kN.m) under pure torsion, as per IS 456:2000, is _________ (round off to one decimal place).
explanation:
Given:

Beam width, $ B = 250 \, \text{mm} $

Effective depth, $ d = 500 \, \text{mm} $

Design shear strength, $ \tau_c = 0.3 \, \text{N/mm}^2 $

The effective shear force, $ V_e $, can be calculated as:

$ V_e = \tau_c \times B \times d = 0.3 \times 250 \times \left(\frac{500}{1000}\right) = 37.50 \, \text{kN} $

In the relationship given by IS 456:2000, the ultimate shear $ V_{ue} $ is:

$ V_{ue} = V_u + \frac{1.6 \times T_u}{B} = 37.5 $

Solving for the torsional moment capacity $ T_u $:

$ T_u = \frac{37.5 \times 0.25}{1.60} = 5.86 \, \text{kN.m} $
